death.instructions: Narrate the player feeling lost in a realm of complete darkness, and in the distance, they suddenly see a shadowy figure. The figure stands facing you for a long moment, before it begins to run towards you, reaching out as if to consume you. Right before the shadow reaches you, you awake in a cold sweat. Narrate the player character or party awakening in a different location after a near-death experience. Have an NPC be present to explain what happened to the player character or party after they lost consciousness: how they nearly died but were saved, brought to their present location and treated. Prefer mundane explanations. The character or party may have lost some or all of their possessions, which may have been looted or stolen or even taken by a trophy by a nemesis. This brush with death may leave a superficial physical mark on the characters (scars, mild deformities, etc.), and it should always have narrative consequences (for example, a loss of confidence in the player character or party's abilities), but the player character or party should be recovered to to full health and able to continue their adventure right away. If narratively appropriate, the character may be maimed significantly--but only to a degree that they could be fully restored later in the story by a prosthesis or training so as make their disability to no longer be any obstacle. Examples: losing an arm or a leg can be replaced; a protective suit and recurring bacta treatments can eschew the lasting damage of severe burns; expensive prosthetic eyes/ears or training in the ways of the Force can give a player 'second sight/hearing' that could more than overcome lost sight/hearing. If a player is maimed, they should recieve a restoration/recovery question immediately upon waking that--when completed--will restore them to full or slightly improved functioning/ability.
